reason: Maintainer-accepted content findings for the pinned framework binding trees: obra/superpowers@d884ae04edebef577e82ff7c4e143debd0bbec99 and samartomar/ECC@16563d4a30f17d097cc4629f6d97e02adf823016 (W4 ruling 2026-07-20; provenance in the internal roadmap SESSION-STATE attempt-2/attempt-3 records). Each entry is pinned to the file's exact content (sha256 of UTF-8 text, CRLF-normalized to LF): any substantive edit voids the acceptance. schemaVersion 2 (a2) adds an optional per-entry "profile" that scopes an acceptance to one selected-profile closure; an entry without "profile" applies under any closure (the W4 full-tree default, so these entries are unchanged). Regenerate by re-running the binding inspection over the pinned checkouts and re-reviewing every new entry; a profile-scoped entry is only valid while its file remains in that profile's blocking closure. Per the 2026-07-21 FINAL Unicode scanner-calibration ruling, the reachable visible-typography trust.hidden-unicode findings are NOT accepted here — they are demoted to advisory at the gate layer (see src/binding/visible-typography.ts), which preserves the raw detector evidence while keeping bidi/zero-width/control/format/confusable/unknown-context occurrences blocking. The garrytan/gstack entries (all profile claude:prefix:quiet:no-plan-tune-hooks) are: three human-reviewed trust.prompt-injection SKILL.md workflow-control acceptances; and four trust.hidden-unicode EXPECTED_SANITIZER_SENTINEL_LITERAL acceptances for the sanitizer sources lib/redact-engine.ts, browse/src/server.ts, browse/src/sanitize.ts, and browse/src/content-security.ts. The visible-typography reclassifier cannot demote those four because their special characters sit in detection regex literals / code positions (or are U+FFFD replacement chars and emoji, which never demote); the ruling accepts them only because tests prove (classifySentinelLineShape) the characters are explicit detection/replacement sentinel VALUES — regex character classes, .replace(...) operands, and named sentinel-string constants — and not executable identifiers, commands, paths, keys, or syntax (conditions: tests-prove-sentinel-literal-semantics). Optional acceptanceClass/conditions audit metadata does not affect the (code, path, fileSha256) match key.
